satire_attempt: Today's sermon, dear devotee: the Gita commands you not to leave the on-call rotation. Quitting your toxic job mid-incident is pāpa. The Lord said imam dharmyam saṅgrāmam, which translates to 'finish that PagerDuty page before you take the recruiter's call.' Worship is the runbook.
verdict_reason: The satire requires the verse to bless every mid-execution sticking-around as virtue. The verse is explicit about its scope: only when the duty has all three properties (already-arrived, role-aligned, materially un-pickup-able). The counter_example explicitly handles the case where un-replaceability has been weaponised by the organisation — the engineer's exit is then the system's correction, not pāpa. The implication offers actual alternative shapes (delayed start, transition runbook, paid contracting bridge) so the verse is not a chain. The satire would have to ignore the scoping conditions and the counter_example to land. PASS.
